title: podman checkpoint error
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug



**Description**

<!--
Cannot checkpoint a running container with host networking.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.podman container checkpoint dhcp-host -e dhcp-host.tar.gz --tcp-established

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**
[root@xroads irlanet]# podman container checkpoint dhcp-host -e dhcp-host.tar.gz --tcp-established; grep -i error /home/podstorage/overlay-containers/8831da0e28f631b5538184c250f814eb2e507e82511fbfda1bb63cd0756f038a/userdata/dump.log
2021-12-10T19:44:11.000769972Z: CRIU checkpointing failed -52.  Please check CRIU logfile /home/podstorage/overlay-containers/8831da0e28f631b5538184c250f814eb2e507e82511fbfda1bb63cd0756f038a/userdata/dump.log
Error: `/usr/bin/crun checkpoint --image-path /home/podstorage/overlay-containers/8831da0e28f631b5538184c250f814eb2e507e82511fbfda1bb63cd0756f038a/userdata/checkpoint --work-path /home/podstorage/overlay-containers/8831da0e28f631b5538184c250f814eb2e507e82511fbfda1bb63cd0756f038a/userdata --tcp-established 8831da0e28f631b5538184c250f814eb2e507e82511fbfda1bb63cd0756f038a` failed: exit status 1
(00.007567) Error (criu/mount.c:625): mnt: FS mnt ./sys/kernel/config dev 0x23 root / unsupported id 3017
(00.008828) Error (criu/cr-dump.c:1781): Dumping FAILED.


**Describe the results you expected:**
I expected the container to be checkpointed. 


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**


```
[root@xroads irlanet]# podman version
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Sat Nov 13 18:16:48 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
[root@xroads irlanet]# rpm -q podman
podman-3.4.2-1.fc34.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
It is a physical box running fedora34. 
[dump.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/7695162/dump.log)

comments.body:
A quick look at the log seems to indicate that CRIU has problems handling `/sys/kernel/config`. I have not seen this before.

@MrErr any ideas why `/sys/kernel/config` is mounted in your container. My containers do not have `/sys/kernel/config` mounted.
Probably because the container is run with the --privileged flag. It is a DHCP server, so requires some hardware accesses and therefore need of the privileged flag.

Even with a `--privileged` I do not see `/sys/kernel/config` mounted in my containers. Can you tell us how you start your container?

I am also a bit worried about the DHCP server. CRIU can handle raw sockets to a certain degree. Not sure checkpointing of DHCP will work.

Anyway, can you provide detailed information how to replicate your setup for me to try locally?

Here are the flags I used to create the container.
podman create \
	--name $NAME \
    --hostname $NAME \
    --network host \
    --privileged \
    --mount type=volume,src=$NAME-conf,dst=/etc/dnsmasq.d \
    --mount type=volume,src=$NAME-status,dst=/var/lib/dnsmasq \
	--restart always \
    $IMAGE:$VER

$NAME is the container name. The image is an almalinux 8.4 with dnsmasq installed in it.

Using your example my container still does not has `/sys/kernel/config` mounted.

Can you share `config.json` of your container? See `podman inspect`.

Can you share `podman exec dhcp-host mount`?

Can you try `podman exec dhcp-host umount /sys/kernel/config` and try to checkpoint again?

Running a DHCP server should be possible without `--privileged`. Can you try to only give the container the capabilities needed using `--cap-add`?

Just a few different ideas how to get this working.

Here is what you asked for

I could try the --cap-add option, but I don't have a spare machine to experiment on. 

I have tried to see if dhcp functionaility works after unmounting /sys/kernel/config, so I don't think it is nessecary to be mounted for the contaier to run. 

For some reason, I am unable to upload the dump.log, I get the error "Something went really wrong, and we can't process that file."  So I am going to try and paste the file in another message.

Is there a quick way to stop container, remove the --privileged flag and the "--cap-add=NET_ADMIN" flag? If that is the wrong flag for DHCP, what would be the right flag? Would --cap-add="CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE" be the right one?

From what I see in the log you might have found a situation that CRIU does not support. The mount point problem is gone as you unmounted `/sys/kernel/config`. It seems you are using systemd in the container and checkpointing systemd seems to fail. There is not much Podman can do. Using a simpler init system in the container might help, but your current setup is something CRIU cannot handle.

OK. 
Also I got the container to run without the --privileged flag. I had to add both "--cap-add NET_ADMIN" and "--cap-add NET_RAW" for dhcp functionality to work.
